item.title: India to Unveil Broader Gauge for Inflation in Data Revamp
item.description: India is set to launch a Producer Price Index for the first time, a step toward aligning with most advanced economies, giving policymakers a broader measure of inflation that captures price pressures across both goods and services in the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

item.title: Philip Morris Cuts Outlook on $500 Million Canada Writedown
item.description: Philip Morris International Inc. cut its profit forecast for this fiscal year after writing down the value of its investment in its Canadian affiliate by $500 million.

item.title: Goldman Said to Work on Retailer IPO in Boost to Its Kuwait Push
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is working on a potential initial public offering of a Kuwaiti online beauty and fashion retailer, a deal that could mark the Wall Street bank’s first listing mandate in the Gulf nation as it seeks to expand there.

item.title: Cambodia Seeks Rare UN Ruling in $300 Billion Thai Energy Spat
item.description: Cambodia has initiated rare United Nations compulsory conciliation proceedings against Thailand to resolve a decades-long maritime border dispute, after talks collapsed over an area both countries say holds $300 billion worth of oil and gas.

item.title: SK Hynix to Double Wafer Capacity to Ease Memory Chip Crunch
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. plans to double its memory chip wafer capacity over the coming half-decade, a major expansion that should help ease a global shortage of an essential component of AI.

item.title: Arm May Hit $15 Billion AI Chip Revenue Goal Early, CEO Says
item.description: Arm Holdings Plc may achieve its target of $15 billion in sales of its own chips earlier than anticipated, its chief executive officer said, reflecting stronger-than-projected demand from the AI boom.

item.title: STMicro Nearly Doubles Outlook for 2026 Data Center Revenue
item.description: STMicroelectronics NV nearly doubled its forecast for revenue from its data centers business to $1 billion this year, fueled by booming demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: China’s Lab-Grown Diamonds Emerge as Unlikely Winner in AI Boom
item.description: China’s lab-grown diamonds are emerging as a surprising beneficiary of the artificial intelligence boom, with demand climbing while they gain traction as a key component in advanced chipmaking.

item.title: Commodities in ‘Super-Squeeze’ as Hormuz Risks Build, HSBC Says
item.description: Commodities are in a “super-squeeze” that will worsen if the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut, according to HSBC Holdings Plc.

item.title: RBI May Have Sold Gold to Save FX Reserves, BE Analysis Shows
item.description: India’s central bank may have offloaded a portion of its gold holdings to shield its foreign-currency assets from the cascading fallout of the war in the Middle East, according to an analysis by Bloomberg Economics based on publicly available data.

item.title: The Iran War Is Pushing the Global Gas Trade Into the Shadows
item.description: Running out of time and patience, major producers like Qatar are ripping up the maritime rulebook — with potentially lasting consequences.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Drawn Into Probe Over €5.7 Billion Bolloré Sale
item.description: Morgan Stanley risks being embroiled in a French criminal case by a group of communities in Africa that suspect the US bank helped Vincent Bolloré launder money stemming from the disposal of an allegedly corrupt €5.7 billion ($6.6 billion) asset.

item.title: Global Funds Pull Out a Decade’s Worth of India Equity Inflows
item.description: Overseas investors’ cumulative net equity investments in India have slid to a near-decade low after relentless selling, highlighting the fading attractiveness of the nation’s $4.9 trillion stock market.

item.title: FTSE 100 Lags as AI Enthusiasm Boosts European Stocks
Eurazeo Raises €3.9 Billion for Flagship Direct Lending Fund
item.description: French asset manager Eurazeo SE has raised €3.9 billion ($4.5 billion) for its latest flagship direct lending fund, another sign that investors are gravitating to Europe as the private credit boom enters a more uncertain phase in the US.

item.title: State Street Head Trader Says Won May Rebound in Second Half
item.description: The South Korean won could rebound in the second half of the year as foreign selling of local equities eases and persistent current-account surpluses support the currency, according to a State Street Bank and Trust Co. trader with more than 25 years at the firm.

item.title: Twinco Capital Raises $190 Million for Supply-Chain Financing
item.description: European fintech firm Twinco Capital has raised a total of €165 million ($190 million) in a series B equity round and a securitization fund.

item.title: China’s Solar Industry Launches Space Alliance With Few Details
item.description: Chinese solar manufacturers and research institutes launched an alliance on Tuesday to promote solar energy in space, a potential new avenue for growth in an industry beset by terrestrial overcapacity.

item.title: Russian Aircraft Production Surges as Putin Pivots to Drone War
item.description: Even as the Kremlin’s war machine starts to lose steam after years of relentless expansion, Russia’s aircraft sector is on a tear, fueled by insatiable demand for thousands of drones.

item.title: SpaceX — To Boldly Raise Money Where No IPO Has Gone Before
item.description: The final frontier is efficiency, as some of the world’s biggest companies land on indexes.

item.title: Poland Set to Hold Interest Rates After Inflation Pressure Cools
item.description: Poland’s central bank is poised to keep interest rates unchanged for a third straight month as an unexpected drop in the inflation rate relieves pressure to tighten monetary policy.

item.title: China’s State Buyer Seeks Scrutiny of New Fortescue Iron Ore
item.description: China’s state-backed iron ore buyer has instructed some steel mills to ask questions about Fortescue Ltd.’s new low-grade product as negotiations over a long-term supply contract hit a rough patch.

item.title: Schroders Renewable Unit Targets AI Assets as Power Demand Soars
item.description: Renewable-energy investor Schroders Greencoat is targeting newer data center-linked assets as AI fuels a surge in electricity demand.

item.title: Pimco Says Treasury Yields Driven by Fed Bets, Not AI, for Now
item.description: A boom in artificial intelligence-related borrowing may become a bigger influence on bond markets over time, but the idea that it’s behind a recent rise in long-dated Treasury yields appears overstated, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

item.title: Japan’s 10-Year Bond Yield Drops After Auction Sees Firm Demand
item.description: Japan’s 10-year government bond yield dropped after an auction of the tenor drew firm demand as investors were attracted to high yields despite the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East.

item.title: Japan’s Duty-Free Sales Climb in May as Tourism Boom Endures
item.description: Japan’s duty-free sales surged in May as tourists flock to the country to take advantage of a persistently weak yen, helping major department stores offset a sharp decline in visitors from China.

item.title: Nvidia Boss Says Workers Should Be Paid ‘as Much as Possible’
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said he pays his workers as much as possible, weighing in on a growing global debate about how profits from the AI infrastructure boom should be shared.

item.title: Tencent Jumps After Report It’s Set to Launch WeChat AI Agent
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. shares advanced the most in more than three years after a report it’s testing a prototype AI agent fueled optimism about the company’s artificial intelligence efforts.

item.title: PBOC Cuts Cash Operation to Record Low as Bond Rally Deepens
item.description: China’s central bank reduced the size of its daily open-market operation to a record low, extending efforts to absorb excess cash as a rally in bonds drove benchmark yields to their lowest since August.

item.title: China’s LNG Imports Rebound in May as Buyers Prepare for Summer
item.description: China’s liquefied natural gas imports rebounded in May as the world’s largest buyer stepped up purchases ahead of peak summer demand, reversing a months-long decline following disruptions to Middle East supplies.
